---
title: "Section 3 Exploratory Data Analysis Exercise"
author: "My Ha"
format: docx
editor: visual
---

# **Exploratory Data Analysis**

**Section: 02**

**Name: My Ha**

## References

To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>

EDA Publication from “The Journal of Open Source Software”

Link:

![](JOSS%20Image%20for%20insertion.png){fig-align="left"}

<https://joss.theoj.org/papers/10.21105/joss.01509>

## Install Packages- comment out when complete

```{r}
#install.packages(c("rlang", "vctrs", "pillar", "tibble", "dplyr"))
```

## Install Libraries

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)
```

## Set Working Directory and load file into that location

```{r}
setwd("C:/Users/hahoa/OneDrive/Desktop/Augustana/DATA 332")
getwd()

```

## Load data set

```{r}
Section3Data<-read_excel("Data332_Section_3_Data.xlsx")

glimpse(Section3Data)
# Use the Global environment to view Section3Data
```

# **Univariate Analyses**

## Summary Statistics

```{r}
summary(Section3Data$Revenue)
```

## Distribution Analysis

### Histogram

```{r}
ggplot(Section3Data, aes(x = Revenue)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
  labs(title = "Histogram of Revenue",
       x = "Revenue",
       y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
```

### Density Plot

```{r}
ggplot(Section3Data, aes(x = Revenue)) +
  geom_density(fill = "darkgreen", alpha = 0.6) +
  labs(title = "Density Plot of Revenue",
       x = "Revenue",
       y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal()

```

## Outlier Identification

Box Plots

```{r}
ggplot(Section3Data, aes(y = "", x = Revenue)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "orange", alpha = 0.7) +
  labs(title = "Boxplot of Revenue",
       x = "",
       y = "Revenue") +
  theme_minimal()
```

# **Bivariate Analyses**

## Correlations

```{r}
Section3Data %>%
  select(Revenue, Quantity, Costs, Profit) %>%
  cor(use = "complete.obs")
```

```{r}
Revenue_v_Profit <- ggplot(Section3Data, aes(x = Revenue, y = Profit)) +
  geom_point(color = "steelblue", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Revenue vs Profit",
    x = "Revenue",
    y = "Profit"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
print(Revenue_v_Profit)
```

```{r}
# Regression Model
model <- lm(Profit ~ Revenue, data = Section3Data)
summary(model)
print(model)
```

## Covariance

data standardization is required to compare Magnitude of Covariance

```{r}
cov(Section3Data[, c("Quantity", "Revenue", "Costs", "Profit")], 
    use = "complete.obs")

```

```{r}
cov_mat <- cov(Section3Data[, c("Quantity", "Revenue", "Costs", "Profit")]) 
cov_df <- melt(cov_mat) 

ggplot(cov_df, aes(Var1, Var2, fill = value)) + geom_tile() + 
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#3498DB", high = "#E74C3C", mid = "white", midpoint = 0) + 
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) + 
  labs(title = "Covariance Matrix Heatmap", fill = "Covariance")

```

## Ellipse Plot

```{r}
ggplot(Section3Data, aes(Costs, Profit)) + 
  stat_ellipse(type = "norm", color = "#2980B9", linewidth = 1) + 
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) + 
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) + 
  labs(title = "Covariance Ellipse Plot")

```

```{r}
 
# A narrow, tilted ellipse → strong covariance.
# A wide, circular ellipse → weak covariance.

```
